title: cladocolea coriacea
intro: Cladocolea coriacea, also known as the forest fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Cladocolea coriacea can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
